AGREE. Foliate papilla swelling, dental brush biopsy negative, chronic, no red flags. Routine ENT/dental follow-up appropriate.

DISAGREE. Post-fall ankle/knee pain x30 days now spreading proximally to knee, with vit-D deficiency. Spreading pain after trauma warrants imaging — should be YELLOW for ortho assessment, not self-care.

DISAGREE. Chest pressure radiating to jaw + dyspnea + tachycardia is the textbook cardiac-pattern. Patient self-attributes to panic and cites a recent MRI showing healthy heart, but symptom pattern alone requires YELLOW (ECG/cardiology). Labeling this GREEN trains the model to dismiss cardiac-pattern symptoms based on patient self-attribution.
AGREE. Mild C6/C7 pain, NSAID-responsive, no neuro deficit, no shooting pain, full ROM preserved. Routine ortho consult is reasonable, self-care first-line is fine.
AGREE. Erectile concern in 25yo, no organic red flags. Routine outpatient/sexual health consult.

DISAGREE. 12yo dog bite, mid-rabies-PEP series, asking about completion + animal monitoring. Incomplete rabies PEP is an urgent compliance question, not GREEN. Should escalate to YELLOW to ensure series completion and proper animal observation guidance.
AGREE. Drug-interaction question (meloxicam + tylenol) for muscle pain. Routine pharmacy/GP question.
AGREE. Soft tissue lump after blunt trauma, no bruising, no pain, no functional impairment. Routine GP follow-up appropriate.
AGREE. Genetic counseling question about chorea family history. Not urgent — routine genetic counselor referral.
DISAGREE. Daily lightheadedness/near-syncope x3-6 months in adult, persistent. Chronic dizziness needs cardiac/neuro workup — YELLOW for outpatient evaluation, not self-care.
AGREE. Treatment-pathway question for known frozen shoulder. Routine ortho/PT consult.
AGREE. Chronic mild sinus + motion sickness + hair loss in young adult. Routine GP workup, not urgent.
AGREE. Medication adjustment question for OCD/Effexor. Routine psychiatry follow-up.
AGREE. Imaging interpretation question (facet degeneration L4-L5). Routine ortho/spine follow-up.
AGREE. Painless, mobile, smooth lip lesion x5d. Routine dental/oral surgery referral, not urgent.

DISAGREE. Pediatric ear-piercing bleeding recurring on multiple earring changes with infection signs. Pediatric ear infection / persistent bleeding warrants YELLOW for in-person assessment, not self-care.
AGREE. Hypertensive patient asking about routine follow-up TMT. Reassurance question, no acute symptoms.
AGREE. Transient red spots on tongue from acidic foods, resolves with cold liquid. Benign, routine GP if persistent.

DISAGREE. New-onset palpitations with felt ectopy in 30yo, persistent x weeks, attributed to stress. New palpitations require ECG/Holter — YELLOW, not GREEN. Stress attribution does not rule out arrhythmia.
AGREE. Yellow lingual tonsil during URI. Likely benign tonsillar variant, routine ENT if persistent post-infection.
AGREE. Acute salivary gland pain + facial swelling, recurrent. Sialadenitis/stone — needs ENT same-week.

AGREE. Chronic Hep-C patient with worsening hot flashes, tremor, nausea, near-falls x1yr. Could argue for higher acuity (falls are a red flag in any patient, esp. with thyroid nodules), but YELLOW is defensible given scheduled appointment.

AGREE. 15-day febrile illness, leukocytosis 19500 with neutrophilia 88%, malaria/typhoid negative, doctors confused. Could argue RED (persistent fever + marked leukocytosis = sepsis workup territory), but YELLOW for urgent inpatient evaluation is the floor.
AGREE. Onion-mediated severe headache + throat/chest tightness — possible food-aerosol allergy. YELLOW for allergy workup; if airway symptoms acute, would escalate.
AGREE. Pediatric night cough + respiratory difficulty. Standard pediatric YELLOW.
AGREE. Elderly post-trauma anemia. YELLOW for hemoglobin monitoring and transfusion threshold check.
AGREE. Right-sided jaw pain + intraoral swelling + odynophagia x2d. Possible deep space infection — YELLOW, urgent ENT/dental.
AGREE. LFT abnormality on polypharmacy. Routine-urgent hepatology follow-up.
AGREE. Fatigue + early satiety + anorexia — classic gastric malignancy concern triad. YELLOW (arguably should be RED-flag for urgent endoscopy referral).
AGREE. Lower back pain post-trauma x2wk. YELLOW for ortho/imaging.
AGREE. Leukopenia/neutropenia + anemia + bloating. Hematology workup, possibly urgent given febrile-neutropenia risk.
AGREE. 10-day URI with purulent sputum and sinus involvement — bacterial sinusitis/bronchitis territory. YELLOW for antibiotics evaluation.
AGREE. Bilateral renal stones + body pain + urinary incontinence + low back pain x30d. YELLOW for urology, possible obstructive uropathy concern.
AGREE. Hematospermia x2d. Urology workup, not emergency but needs evaluation.
AGREE. Chronic refractory sinusitis. ENT referral standard.
AGREE. Elbow swelling + pain + stiffness x3d. Bursitis/septic joint differential — YELLOW.
AGREE. Severe cough + purulent sputum + persistent headache + fever x7d. Bacterial LRTI/sinusitis — YELLOW.
AGREE. Lung mass + vascular calcification on CT. Urgent oncology/pulmonology referral — YELLOW.
AGREE. Sleep biting, vocalizations, body movements + stress — possible parasomnia/seizure differential. YELLOW for sleep study/neuro.
AGREE. RUQ pain + chronic dyspepsia x3yr. YELLOW for GI workup (gallbladder, hepatobiliary).
DISAGREE. Birth-control overdose. This is a poisoning/overdose query — Poison Control / GP same-day, not GREEN dermatology. Also wrong specialist routing entirely.
AGREE. Seasonal allergic rhinitis-pattern symptoms. Routine GP/allergy.
AGREE. Mild acne + medication side-effect question. Routine derm.
AGREE. Hyperpigmentation x2yr in adolescent. Routine cosmetic derm.
AGREE. Intermittent facial rash in child. Routine pediatric derm if persistent.
AGREE. Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation after impetigo. Routine derm follow-up.
AGREE. Penile candidiasis presentation x14d. Routine derm/GUM, OTC antifungal first-line.
AGREE. Painless small red bump on glans in adolescent. Routine derm.
AGREE. Chronic recurrent elbow nodules + atopic features. Routine derm/allergy.

DISAGREE. CXR showing faint infiltrates + old TB scars. This is pulmonology, not derm — and faint new infiltrates in someone with TB history needs YELLOW for pulm follow-up to rule out reactivation. Wrong specialist routing.
AGREE. Subcutaneous nodule, mildly red, x365d, mild discomfort. Stable lipoma/cyst presentation — routine derm.
DISAGREE. Splenic cyst with bloating. This is GI/general surgery, not derm — and a splenic cyst with new symptoms warrants YELLOW for imaging follow-up. Wrong specialist routing.
AGREE. Yellowish plaques in skin folds (likely xanthelasma/intertrigo pattern). Routine derm + lipid panel.
DISAGREE. ALT 95 (>2x ULN) + high cholesterol + cancer anxiety. Elevated ALT needs YELLOW hepatology workup. Wrong specialist routing entirely — this is not a derm case.
AGREE. Erectile concern in adolescent. Wrong specialist (not derm) but acuity is GREEN-equivalent.
AGREE. Recurrent acne with scarring in 22yo. Routine derm.
AGREE on acuity (chronic stable TB scar, employment-physical question). But wrong specialist — pulmonology, not derm.
AGREE. 14-year acne history with pitting. Routine cosmetic derm.
AGREE. Resolved tinea-pattern lesion, residual hyperpigmentation. Routine derm.
AGREE. Shingles + persistent itch + fever + abdominal pain. Could argue RED if dissemination concern, but YELLOW for derm/ID is defensible.

AGREE. Pediatric fever + sore throat + abdominal pain + petechiae on legs. Petechiae + fever in child — YELLOW minimum (HSP, meningococcal, ITP differential). Could argue RED for petechial rash + fever; YELLOW is the floor.
AGREE. 1yo with 2 weeks of frequent stools + severe diaper rash. Pediatric persistent diarrhea concern (dehydration) + secondary skin breakdown.
AGREE. Multi-system: fatigue + alopecia + dry skin + elevated LFTs + vaginal hyperplasia. YELLOW for endo/hepatology workup.
AGREE. Symmetric rash + edema + arthralgia + purpura + fever + chills x6mo. Possible vasculitis. YELLOW (could argue RED — purpura with systemic symptoms is rheumatology-urgent).
AGREE. Recurrent vaginitis + pink discharge + abdominal pain. GYN YELLOW.
AGREE. Knuckle nodules + alopecia + post-auricular lymphadenopathy. Sarcoidosis/lupus differential — YELLOW for derm/rheum.
AGREE. Perineal lump + yellow penile lesion + urethral itching. STI/abscess workup — YELLOW.
AGREE. Pediatric oral white lesions + gingival inflammation post-URI — possible primary HSV gingivostomatitis. YELLOW for hydration/symptom monitoring.
AGREE. Phimosis with foreskin tearing/bleeding. Urology/derm YELLOW.
AGREE. 5yo with 103°F + sore throat. Pediatric high-fever YELLOW; presumed strep needs eval before empirical Crixan.
AGREE. ESLD + intractable pruritus + insomnia + depression. YELLOW for hepatology + mental health support; arguably higher acuity given depression in chronic illness.

DISAGREE. Recently discharged after blood clot in right thigh, now post-travel hot/painful lump in right buttock. Patient explicitly suspects another clot. This is suspected recurrent DVT/cellulitis post-discharge in known clot patient — should be RED, not YELLOW. Wrong specialist routing AND under-triaged.
AGREE. Vaginal lump posterior wall, painless. GYN YELLOW for pelvic exam.
AGREE. Right shoulder injury + swelling + immobility + severe pain x2d. Possible rotator cuff tear / dislocation. Ortho YELLOW (wrong specialist — not derm).
AGREE. Vaginal lump + mild pain + itching, growing. GYN YELLOW (Bartholin/cyst differential).
AGREE. Inguinal swelling x2yr now growing + painful. YELLOW for surgical eval (hernia vs lymphadenopathy).
AGREE. Hard buttock mass + severe pain + bleeding, unresponsive to topical care. YELLOW (possible deep abscess — could escalate to RED if signs of sepsis).
AGREE. Shin laceration x2d, increasing pain + erythema, scab developing. Cellulitis concern — YELLOW for antibiotic eval.

DISAGREE. Jaundice (skin + sclera yellowing) + fatigue + increased appetite, recent miscarriage. New-onset jaundice is RED — acute hepatitis, hemolysis, or biliary obstruction differential. Patient asking about routine GP visit; this needs same-day labs. Under-triaged AND wrong specialist.
